statement.latex: The function$d_n ( , ) =(1/n) |\{x \{1, ,n\} (x) (x)\}|$is a distance on the symmetric group~$S_n$. For a finitely generated group$G$, an is a sequence of set-theoretic maps$f_n : G S_n$satisfying$d_n (f_n(g)f_n(h), f_n(gh)) 0$as$n$for all$g, h G$. An almost-homomorphism$\{f_n\}$is said to be if there is a sequence of group homomorphisms$_n : G S_n$such that$d_n ( _n(g), f_n(g)) 0$as$n$for all$g G...
